description: LLM generation provider preset catalog + instance-level credential storage contract. Design doc: docs/backend/generation-provider-design.md sections 8-10; decision change D-2026-07-08-KEY (keys may now be stored encrypted in DB, superseding the env-only rule).
status: designed 2026-07-08, implementation pending. The catalog below is the value domain for GENERATION_PROVIDER and llm_provider_credentials.provider; until implemented, runtime still accepts only openai_compatible|minimax.
core_rule: The catalog is UI prefill data shipped with the code, not a security boundary and not a capability gate: base_url stays editable, model names stay free-form, and whether a combination works is decided by POST /api/generation/ping and the real generation chain. Plaintext keys never enter Git, sync feeds, manual sync packages, audit details or any API response; at-rest storage is Fernet-encrypted only.
client_rule: Every preset goes through the single OpenAI-compatible chat/completions client (backend/app/llm/provider.py request_chat_completion) with the existing /chat/completions URL completion rule. No provider-specific client branches: anthropic uses its OpenAI-compatible endpoint; native x-api-key or non-chat/completions protocols are out of scope (use custom + a gateway).

catalog.name: Anthropic Claude
catalog.notes: Uses Anthropic's OpenAI-compatible endpoint; the native x-api-key protocol is intentionally not a second client branch.

catalog.name: MiniMax
catalog.notes: Current instance default provider; default model MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed stays unchanged.

catalog.name: Ollama (本地/自托管)
catalog.notes: Self-hosted; key input may stay empty.

catalog.name: 自定义（兜底 base_url + key）
catalog.notes: Catch-all for any OpenAI-compatible gateway/proxy: base_url is mandatory and free-form. Legacy GENERATION_PROVIDER=openai_compatible is a deprecated alias of custom.

catalog_rules: common_models are dropdown hints only; the server never validates model names against them (generation_policy.model / GENERATION_MODEL accept any value).
Catalog changes are contract JSON edits shipped with a release; there is no runtime edit API.
GET /api/generation/providers serves this catalog verbatim to any logged-in user; the response contains no credential data.

credential_table.scope: instance-level (no workspace_code): credentials are instance assets, workspaces reference them via generation_policy.credential_id
columns: provider (str32, one of catalog codes incl. custom)
base_url (str512, resolved at write time: catalog default or user-edited; mandatory for custom)
key_encrypted (text, Fernet token; empty string allowed for key-less providers such as ollama)
key_last4 (str8, plaintext last 4 chars for masked display; display path never decrypts)
enabled (bool, default true; DELETE = soft-disable with disabled_at)

encryption.algorithm: Fernet (cryptography), key derived via HKDF-SHA256 from AUTH_SESSION_SECRET with salt 'infowatchtower/llm-credentials/v1' and info 'fernet-key'; no new env variable
encryption.rotation: MultiFernet over Settings.auth_session_secret_list (AUTH_SESSION_SECRETS rotation list, first = current): encrypt with the first key, decrypt tries all; on startup rows decryptable only with an old key are re-encrypted idempotently. Dropping an old secret without the rotation list makes stored credentials undecryptable: behavior is degrade-to-unconfigured (key_source=credential_missing, rule-fallback drafts, ping key_missing), audited as generation.credential.decrypt_failed, never a crash and never silent env fallback.

credential_table.masking: Every API response (list, detail, policy resolved view, ping, errors) exposes at most key_masked = '****' + key_last4; neither plaintext nor the Fernet ciphertext ever appears in API responses or audit details.
credential_table.sync_export_exclusion: llm_provider_credentials never enters sync feeds, manual sync packages or any export; secrets are re-entered per environment (pull-only intranet topology keeps its own rows).

resolution_priority: 1. workspace generation_policy.credential_id set -> enabled credential row decrypted: provider/base_url/key come from the row (key_source=credential); disabled/missing/undecryptable -> treat as key-not-configured (key_source=credential_missing), never silently fall back to env

2. credential_id null -> instance env chain byte-identical to current behavior: GENERATION_API_KEY_REF -> GENERATION_API_KEY -> MINIMAX_API_KEY (provider/base_url via GENERATION_*/MINIMAX_* compatibility chain)
3. model/temperature/max_tokens/timeout plus budget and fallback_behavior keep the existing policy-over-env layering regardless of key source
api: GET /api/generation/credentials (super_admin or editor_admin): masked list only
POST /api/generation/credentials (super_admin): {provider, base_url?, api_key, label}; base_url defaults from catalog, mandatory for custom; audited generation.credential.create
PATCH /api/generation/credentials/{id} (super_admin): label/base_url/enabled/api_key (new key replaces and re-encrypts); audited generation.credential.update
POST /api/generation/ping accepts optional credential_id (takes precedence over workspace_code) for save-then-test; audit detail carries credential_id, never key material
startup_failfast_revision: With DB credentials, 'GENERATION_ENABLED=true and empty env key' downgrades from fail-fast to a startup WARNING (key may be configured at runtime via UI). Kept fail-fast: provider value outside the catalog (openai_compatible allowed as deprecated custom alias) and custom/openai_compatible without GENERATION_BASE_URL. deployment_modes.json startup_failfast_rules must be synced 1:1 when this lands.
invariants: Plaintext keys exist only in env values, credential_ref target files and the outbound Authorization header; the DB stores Fernet ciphertext only.
workspaces.config_json.generation_policy still never contains key/base_url/provider fields; credential_id is a whitelisted pointer, and secret-like payload keys still answer 422.
Provider or credential switching never relaxes generation quality gates, the 10-category constraint or company SQL gating.
The env chain (GENERATION_*/MINIMAX_*) remains fully supported as the fallback path; instances with no credential rows behave byte-identically to the pre-R2 implementation.
